description_long: This obelisk is a tall, monolithic structure with a smooth, greenish surface, tapering to a pointed tip at the top. The shaft is mounted on a stepped, square pedestal that consists of three distinct layers, each slightly smaller than the one below, giving it a tiered appearance. The front face of the base features a recessed rectangular panel with three horizontal bars, suggesting a stylized decorative element. The overall finish is sleek and polished, with sharp, clean edges and a uniform coloration. There are no visible inscriptions or ornate carvings, lending the monument a modern and minimalist aesthetic. The obelisk appears to be constructed from a dense, stone-like material, possibly granite or marble, and is designed to serve as a prominent landmark or commemorative feature in an urban environment.
description: A tall, green, smooth obelisk with a pointed tip and a tiered square base, featuring a recessed panel with horizontal bars on the front. The design is minimalist and modern, with a polished stone-like finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the recessed rectangular panel with three horizontal bars on the base, and the full height of the obelisk.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the plain, unadorned surface of the shaft and the stepped structure of the base.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the side, with no visible panel or ornamentation, highlighting the symmetry of the structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the plain surface and the geometric, tiered base.
attribute_base_design: The base is a stepped, square platform with three distinct layers, each slightly smaller than the one below. The front face features a recessed rectangular panel with three horizontal bars, adding a subtle decorative element. The base appears solid and is made of the same green stone-like material as the shaft.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is sharply pointed, forming a clean pyramid shape with smooth, polished surfaces and no additional decoration.
